title: Vessel with removeable vial
abstract: Disclosed is a vessel (1) comprising a container (10) and a lid (20). The container (10) extends from an open end (11) to a base (12) and comprises a cavity (13) for holding a liquid. The lid (20) closes the open end (11) of the container (10) and comprises: a body portion (21) comprising an annular lip (21a) surrounding a socket (35); and a plug section (23) engageable in the socket (35) to form a water-tight seal therewith. The annular lip (21a) comprises one or more drinking outlets (21b). A sheath (30) is attached to the body portion (21) of the lid and comprises a hollow body (31) extending from the socket (35) into the cavity (13) of the container, wherein the hollow body (31) comprises at least one aperture (32). A vial (40) is attachable to the plug section (23) of the lid, wherein with the plug section engaged in the socket (35), the vial (40) extends from the plug section within the hollow body (31) of the sheath and comprises a vial body (41) having at least one solid section (42) and at least one filter section (43). One or both of the body portion (21) and plug section (23) of the lid are moveable relative to each other between: a closed position wherein the solid section (42) of the vial body covers the aperture (32) in the hollow body (31); and an open position wherein the filter section (43) of the vial body is aligned with the aperture (32) in the hollow body (31).
claims: Claims Claims 1. A vessel comprising: a container extending from an open end to a base and comprising a cavity for holding a liquid; a lid closing the open end of the container and comprising: i. a body portion comprising an annular lip surrounding a socket, the annular lip comprising one or more drinking outlets; and ii. a plug section engageable in the socket to form a water-tight seal therewith; a sheath attached to the body portion of the lid and comprising a hollow body extending from the socket into the cavity of the container, wherein the hollow body comprises at least one aperture; and a vial attachable to the plug section of the lid, wherein with the plug section engaged in the socket, the vial extends from the plug section within the hollow body of the sheath and comprises a vial body having at least one solid section and at least one filter section; wherein the body portion and the plug section of the lid are moveable relative to each other between: a closed position wherein the solid section of the vial body covers the aperture in the hollow body of the sheath; and an open position wherein the filter section of the vial body is aligned with the aperture in the hollow body of the sheath. 2. The vessel as claimed in claim 1 wherein the plug section is engageable in the socket via a clip mechanism and/or interference fit. 3. The vessel as claimed in claim 2 wherein the socket comprises one or more protrusions on a wall thereof; and the plug section comprises one or more recesses on an outer surface thereof shaped and positioned to receive the protrusions when the plug section is engaged in the socket. 4. The vessel as claimed in claim 3 wherein the plug section comprises one or more guiding channels on the outer surface thereof and shaped and positioned to guide the one or more protrusions of the socket into the corresponding recesses of the plug section when the plug section is pushed into the socket. 5. The vessel as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the plug section is rotatable within the socket between the open and closed positions. 6. The vessel as claimed in claim 5 when dependent on claim 4 wherein each guiding channel comprises an additional section leading away from the recess in the direction of rotation. 7. The vessel as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the vial is attachable to the plug section of the lid via a clip mechanism and/or interference fit. 8. The vessel as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the vial body extends in a longitudinal direction from a top end to a bottom end and wherein both the top and bottom ends are closed to contain particulate infusible material within the vial body. 9. The vessel as claimed in claim 8 when dependent on claim 7 wherein the vial body comprises an annular recess located towards the top end, an annular projection located towards the top end, or both; and the plug section comprises an annular flange projecting from an inner surface thereof and dimensioned and positioned to be received in locking engagement with the annular recess and/or annular projection of the vial body. 10. The vessel as claimed in claim 9 wherein the vial body comprises one or more lugs on the outer surface and located towards the top end and which locate in corresponding recesses located on the inner surface of the plug. 11. The vessel as claimed in any one of claims 8 to 10 wherein the at least one solid section and the at least one filter section of the vial body extend parallel to each other in the longitudinal direction of the tubular body at least in an overlap region and the solid section extends around the circumference of the tubular body for a greater distance than the filter section in the overlap region. 12. The vessel as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the annular lip of the body portion of the lid comprises a plurality drinking outlets arranged in an annular configuration. 13. The vessel as claimed in any one of the preceding claims wherein the vial contains particulate infusible material, preferably leaf tea. 14. A method of brewing a beverage with the vessel as claimed in claim 13, comprising the steps of: (i) dosing liquid into the cavity of the container; (ii) attaching the vial to the plug section of the lid; (iii) engaging the plug section in the socket to form the water-tight seal therewith; (iv) closing the open end of the container with the lid; (v) with the body portion and plug section arranged in the open position, allowing the liquid to contact the particulate infusible material through the filter section for a brewing time; and (vi) after the brewing time, moving the body portion and plug section into the closed position. 15. The method as claimed in claim 14 wherein the top and/or bottom end of the vial body comprise an orifice closed by a membrane and the method comprises the additional steps of: removing the vial from the vessel; breaking and/or removing the membrane from the vial; and emptying the particulate infusible material from the vial through the orifice.
